title: 1:1 Paz Santos + Casey Tanaka — 2026-10-05
compiled_truth:
Weekly 1:1 between [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) and [Casey Tanaka](people/casey-tanaka), held over video on Monday morning. Paz is engineering lead; Casey runs developer relations and external documentation at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor). The meeting ran about 35 minutes and covered three main areas: SDK documentation parity, an upcoming conference talk, and some interpersonal friction on the integrations team.

Paz opened by flagging that the Python SDK docs are lagging behind the Rust and Go equivalents by roughly two weeks. Casey acknowledged this but pushed back slightly — the Python bindings themselves only stabilized in late September, and the doc team had been blocked waiting on final API surface confirmations from engineering. They agreed that Casey's team would get a frozen API spec by Wednesday EOD and commit to having draft docs ready for internal review by October 12th. Paz said she'd assign Tomás from her team to be the point person for any technical questions during the doc sprint.

Second topic was Casey's talk at the Lattice Systems conference in November. Casey wants to present a live demo of the single-pass pipeline, but needs access to the staging sandbox with realistic data loads. Paz was hesitant — the staging environment is mid-migration and she doesn't want external-facing demos running against infrastucture that might hiccup. They compromised: Casey will record a backup demo video by October 20th, and Paz will try to have the staging migration wrapped by November 1st so a live demo is feasible as a stretch goal.

The last chunk of the conversation was more sensitive. Casey raised that two members of the integrations squad have been going around the docs team and shipping customer-facing README files that contradict official guidance. Paz seemed surprised but not dismissive. She said she'd talk to the individuals directly and reinforce that all external-facing text needs to go through Casey's review pipeline. Casey was appreciative but also noted this isn't the first time — there's a pattern of engineering treating docs as an afterthought, and she wants Paz to address it structurally, not just case-by-case. Paz agreed to bring it up at the next engineering all-hands.

Overall tone was candid and collaborative. No major tensions between the two — they clearly have a good working dynamic. Casey tends to be more deliberate and process-oriented; Paz is faster-moving and sometimes needs to be reminded of downstream effects on the docs and DevRel side.
timeline: - **2026-09-14** | [Casey Tanaka](people/casey-tanaka) flags Python SDK doc gap in Slack; begins tracking blockers
- **2026-09-28** | [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) confirms Python bindings API surface is frozen for documentation purposes
- **2026-10-05** | This 1:1 meeting held; discussion covers SDK docs, Lattice conference prep, and integrations team friction
- **2026-10-12** | Target date for Casey's team to deliver draft Python SDK docs for internal review
- **2026-10-20** | Deadline for Casey to record backup demo video for Lattice Systems talk
- **2026-11-01** | Paz targets staging environment migration completion to enable live demo option
- **2026-11-15** | Casey presents at Lattice Systems conference on [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) single-pass pipeline
- **2026-12-02** | Follow-up 1:1 reviews whether structural doc-review process changes have taken hold
